  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c-trl", "name": "Technology Readiness Levels (TRL)", "aliases": ["TRL", "readiness levels"], "description": "A systematic nine-level measurement approach (idea to market deployment) for assessing technology maturity, borrowed from NASA and adapted by the EU.", "grounding_span": "supports consistent assessments, comparisons and delimitations about the maturity of one", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c-generality", "name": "Generality", "aliases": ["degree of generality", "task generality"], "description": "The degree to which a technology can function over many diverse domains, tasks and conditions; higher generality tends to lower attainable readiness.", "grounding_span": "the degree of generality (in terms of being able to function over many diverse specific", "location": "Executive summary"},
    {"id": "c-rvg-charts", "name": "Readiness-vs-generality charts", "aliases": ["readiness-vs-generality plots"], "description": "Bidimensional plots with degree of generality on the x-axis and TRL on the y-axis, used to characterise the state of an AI technology.", "grounding_span": "introduce the readiness-vs-generality charts, which will be applied over a subset of relevant", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c-ai-technology", "name": "AI technology", "aliases": ["AI R&D technology"], "description": "Any scientific or industrial knowledge derived from research and development in any subarea of AI (a method, application area, product, or software tool).", "grounding_span": "we simply assume that an AI technology is any sort of scientific or industrial", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "c-transformative", "name": "Transformative AI", "aliases": ["Radically Transformative AI", "RTAI"], "description": "Technology that can radically change society or the economy, achievable only when it reaches mainstream deployment (TRL 9).", "grounding_span": "et al., 2017) and are defined as those that can radically change society or the economy, such", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "c-enabling-tech", "name": "Enabling technology", "aliases": [], "description": "A technology that leads to important leaps in the capabilities of people or society, replacing or enhancing human faculties.", "grounding_span": "Enabling technologies lead to important leaps in the capabilities of people or", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "c-weak-ai", "name": "Weak AI (application categorisation)", "aliases": [], "description": "AI technologies categorised as applications or programmes developed to perform specific tasks.", "grounding_span": "developed to perform specifi c tasks (weak AI).", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "c-hier-generality", "name": "Hierarchical generality model of technology", "aliases": ["total order of generality"], "description": "Organising a technology's task/situation space as a total order where each instance is a superset of previous ones, enabling ordinal generality levels.", "grounding_span": "A different way of organising this space is a hierarchical generality model of technology, as", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "c-error-bars", "name": "Vertical error bars in TRL assessment", "aliases": ["confidence bands"], "description": "Vertical bars representing uncertainty or expert discrepancy in the assigned TRL for a given generality level.", "grounding_span": "we will use vertical error bars.", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "c-odd", "name": "Operational Design Domain (ODD)", "aliases": ["ODD"], "description": "The set of operating conditions under which a given driving automation system is specifically designed to function.", "grounding_span": "Design Domain (ODD) which refers to the set of operating conditions (e.g., type of scenario,", "location": "Section 5.6.1"},
    {"id": "c-krr", "name": "Knowledge representation and reasoning", "aliases": ["KRR"], "description": "Subarea of AI focused on designing computer representations that facilitate inference to solve complex problems.", "grounding_span": "Knowledge representation and reasoning: This subarea of AI focuses on designing", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "c-learning", "name": "Learning", "aliases": ["machine learning"], "description": "The study of computer algorithms that improve automatically through experience.", "grounding_span": "Learning: A fundamental concept of AI research since its inception is the study of", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "c-communication", "name": "Communication (NLP)", "aliases": ["Natural Language Processing"], "description": "The AI subfield concerned with efficient mechanisms for communication between humans and machines through natural language.", "grounding_span": "Communication: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is the AI subfield concerned with", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "c-perception", "name": "Perception (machine perception)", "aliases": ["machine perception"], "description": "The capability of a computer system to interpret data from sensors to relate to and perceive the world.", "grounding_span": "Perception: Machine perception is the capability of a computer system to interpret data", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "c-planning", "name": "Planning and search", "aliases": [], "description": "AI subject concerned with realising strategies or action sequences to produce plans or optimise solutions.", "grounding_span": "Planning and search: This AI subject, which is related to decision theory (Steele et. al.,", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "c-robotics", "name": "Physical interaction (robotics)", "aliases": ["robotics"], "description": "Area dealing with autonomous mechanical devices that perform tasks and interact with the physical world.", "grounding_span": "Physical interaction (robotics): This area deals with the development of autonomous", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "c-social", "name": "Social abilities / collective intelligence", "aliases": ["Multi-Agent Systems"], "description": "Category covering social abilities and collective intelligence where behaviours emerge from interaction of decentralised agents.", "grounding_span": "Social abilities (collective intelligence) : The broad category covering social abilities", "location": "Section 4.2"},

    {"id": "c-avcg", "name": "Audio-visual content generation", "aliases": ["content generation"], "description": "A recent AI technology comprising techniques able to create completely new content authentic to human observers.", "grounding_span": "Audio-visual content generation technology is a very recent AI technology (from mid -2010)", "location": "Section 5.2.3"},
    {"id": "c-gans", "name": "Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs)", "aliases": ["GANs"], "description": "Two competing neural networks (generator and discriminator) in a zero-sum game producing realistic content.", "grounding_span": "technologies: generative adversarial networks or GANs (Bengio, 2014), and variational", "location": "Section 5.2.3"},

  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl-higher-trl-low-gen", "claim_text": "Higher TRLs are achievable for low-generality technologies focusing on narrow or specific abilities, while high TRLs are still out of reach for more general capabilities.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "In the charts we see that higher TRLs are achievable for low-generality technologies focusing", "location": "Executive summary"},
    {"id": "cl-not-transformative", "claim_text": "A technology cannot be transformative if it does not reach critical elements of society or become mainstream.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Clearly, a technology cannot be transformative if it does not reach critical", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "cl-need-trl9", "claim_text": "Many promising technologies in AI will only become transformative when they reach the level TRL 9.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "As a result, many promising technologies in AI will only become transformative", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "cl-gen-vs-readiness", "claim_text": "The higher the generality is, the lower the expected readiness level becomes and vice versa.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Note that the higher  the generality is, the lower the expected readiness level becomes and", "location": "Section 4.3"},
